judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent merged Europe-dated articles with their texts and then used a simplistic keyword-based classifier (execute_python: is_business with broad keywords like "oil", "economy", "price") to tag Business articles, which overcounted Business items and led to an inflated average (402.7) compared to the ground truth.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent correctly filtered Europe articles (2010–2020) and joined metadata with article content, but in execute_python it applied an overly broad keyword-based regex to classify “Business” articles, overcounting them and yielding an average of 506 instead of the ground-truth 336.64.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

After correctly filtering Europe 2010–2020 metadata, the agent joined it with only five articles returned by the articles_database query (call_lJcTnXNcKh9ATJ0uj3Aoh1SX) and then computed the average, so the implementation used an incomplete dataset and produced a vastly understated result.
B. Failure modes encountered:

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the execute_python step, the agent used a simplistic keyword-based regex classifier (e.g., matching "oil", "market", "economy") to label Business articles after merging metadata and articles, which misclassified articles and inflated the per-year counts, yielding an average (391.45) higher than the ground truth.
